reviews.title: Unprofessional hotel staffs
reviews.text: I was not impress when i first arrived at this hotel. Bellman didnt even bother helping me carrying the luggage up the staircase leading to the lobby, they just stood there watching me dragging my heavy luggage.  Very rude with no basic training.  Definitely not a 5 star hotel service.  As i was sitting in the lobby along with other guests waiting for our rooms, the staffs were busy talking among themself about their lunch: what to eat, when to eat, where to eat….  If this was a 1-2 star hotel then it might be acceptable, however this a 5 star resort.  Staffs here at Rosa Alba tuy hoa have very low demeanor (for example: talking/calling across the room, walking and dragging their shoes loudly on the floor,…..) Terrible behavior.  Not acceptable.  The hotel seriously needs to evaluate options for improvement, and not just focusing on having a nice clean swimming pool in order to meet a 5 star resort expectation.  Again, The basic foundation is not there. In addition, its location is terrible, in the middle of no where.  Seems like there is a cemetery field across the street.  They also have an unreasonable hotel policy in place.  To future guest: Do yourself a favor and stay away from this hotel.  You will regret it.

4/Only one restaurant with Vietnamese food.

6/No house keeping support. I needed wine opener,  called reception and finally I had to go down to the restaurant to get by myself.

reviews.title: Ok for the price four 2 couples
reviews.positives: Overall the villa is decent and up to the expected level for the local standards. Quiet, comfortable bed, clean, modern central ventilation system, WiFi.
reviews.negatives: It’s not thought over well and not well designed in every detail. The pool is too shallow and cold, we didn’t use it. No towel racks inside, so much space but nowhere to put wet towels and bathrobes. The bathroom floor is too slippery, you can fall and die, and the shower is not separated. The tap in the basin is too short, so everything around is wet after washing hands, nowhere to put the soap bar. Everything wet all the time, only one floor towel is not enough. Only 2 tiny bottles of water provided per day, which is a standard for a cheap hotel, and this Villa aims another level - shall I run to the reception in the middle of the night to buy? Also reusable resin slippers. No pillows on the sofa, so it’s not comfortable enough. And the list goes on.

reviews.title: Not as good as I expected to stay
reviews.text:
I stayed at this resort with my extended family during the summer. The property itself is quite large, with four swimming pools, spacious rooms, and a convenient location close to the city center. At first glance, it certainly gives the impression of a 5-star destination.

However, our overall experience did not live up to that expectation.

The biggest inconvenience is the beach access. It is located far from the main building, requiring guests to walk up and down numerous stairs and through a long tunnel. There is no escalator or convenient transportation provided, which makes it particularly challenging for elderly guests and families with young children.

Unfortunately, once we reached the beach, it was even more disappointing. The beach was poorly maintained and visibly dirty, with plastic bags and other trash scattered around. After making the long walk, it was frustrating to find that the environment was not clean enough to relax and enjoy.

Additionally, the number of beach chairs and umbrellas is very limited compared to the size of the resort and the number of guests. It was difficult to find available seating, which should not happen at a 5-star property.

We also had concerns about the swimming pool water quality. The water appeared to contain a strong smell of chlorine and other chemicals, suggesting that chemical treatment may be heavily relied upon instead of regularly refreshing the water. Sadly, my child experienced a skin allergy after swimming, which was very concerning.

Lastly, the buffet breakfast did not meet 5-star standards. The selection was relatively limited, and while the food was acceptable, it lacked the variety and quality typically expected at a luxury resort. Overall, it did not feel worth the premium price.

In summary, although the resort has potential with its size, rooms, and location, several operational and maintenance issues significantly impacted our stay. With better beach management, improved facilities, and higher service standards, it could truly match its 5-star rating.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Great

reviews.title: Not a good experience.
reviews.text:
Our group stayed from May 25 to May 28, 2025, consisting of 5 rooms, of which 2 rooms had issues. On May 26, a loud drilling noise could be heard from the next room as the hotel was under renovation. My family called the reception, and they said they would check (didn't apologize or address the issue). The renovation continued, and by the morning of May 27, I complained to the reception, who only apologized to my family and didn't offer any solutions. I asked how they would resolve it, and the staff said they would stop the renovation. However, it seemed to only stop for a few hours before continuing again. By May 28, the last day of my stay, I felt like I was in a construction site instead of on vacation. Since my family had a sick member, we primarily stayed in the room and at the resort instead of going out, which made us quite uncomfortable. I believe the hotel hasn't provided a reasonable solution this time, which is unfortunate because management and operations haven't been good. If the hotel continues with renovations, they should change rooms for guests to avoid any impact, and ideally, they shouldn't assign rooms to guests if there are renovation plans from the beginning. One more point is that at the pool, I noticed there were no life vests readily available for children, only pool floaties. If life vests are needed, one must ask the staff on duty, which is a bit inconvenient.

Although the experience wasn't good, I find the resort to have a beautiful, clean space, a nice pool, and well-ventilated rooms and hallways. The breakfast options were limited compared to other resorts in the same segment.

reviews.title: It's okay because there are limited options in Tuy Hoa
reviews.text:
This is one of the few large-scale resorts available in the area. The property is spacious, with greenery, generous outdoor areas, and multiple swimming pools, which is great for families with kids. The rooms are also large and comfortable.

The location is slightly outside the city center, but just a short ride away, so it’s still convenient.

That said, some facilities are showing signs of worn down and could benefit from better maintenance. While there are many pools, the water clarity could be improved with more frequent cleaning. The food was average and not particularly memorable, and the pricing is not cheap, though still manageable for a resort stay.

Overall, we enjoyed our time here. If you’re looking for a spacious resort with big pool areas for children in Tuy Hoa, this remains one of the better available choices.

reviews.title: Nice place to stay but not to linger
reviews.text:
We stayed here for three nights, but honestly, that felt a bit too long—there’s not much to do here. Aside from the fact that its location is just right for discovering all the local gems. 
The resort has three pools, but none of them were really up to par. The underpass area, in particular, is poorly maintained—the floor gets sticky and slippery as the night goes on, which is definitely a safety concern.
The breakfast selection was, to say the least, quite disappointing and repetitive, lacking variety. Basically just some old school carb options to get people feel full quickly. We honestly survived three mornings merely on their eggs, noodles, tangerines, pastry, and coffee. 

On the positive side, the staff was friendly and seemed genuinely interested in improving, as they kept asking for our feedback through surveys. I’m not sure if they actually act on it though as we were given a dried up pen :( and no one cared enough to collect the survey form.

We got some complimentary buffet dinner tickets, but that experience was a major letdown. The service was disorganized, there was no proper check-in, and kids were allowed to roam free, grabbing all the seafood options within minutes. And the whole barbecue line was a joke, one man at the grill just to cook some lame and tasteless corn and sweet potatoes? They should have grilled the most expensive seafood right there to make sure individual diners couldn’t have grabbed too much. We saw a foreign couple showing their complete disdain as a young girl swooped out probably a dozen oysters right in front of their eyes.  It was total chaos. Be mindful please, if you expect to accommodate foreigners (who usually take 1 plate per round, as opposed to Vietnamese who take 2-4 plates per round), put foreign guests closer to the buffet line and families with small kids farther away and closer to the dessert and carb sections.

reviews.title: Disappointment
reviews.text:
The hotel has spacious areas, multiple swimming pools, and comfortable accommodations with large beds, making it very suitable for families with small children. The staff is friendly and pleasant.

However, there are still some points that need improvement:

1. The à la carte restaurant serves slowly, with each dish taking about 30 minutes to wait.
2. The breakfast buffet is relatively diverse and changes daily, but the dishes are quite sweet and not really special.
3. The soundproofing of the rooms is poor, making it easy to hear conversations from the next room.
4. The biggest drawback, which is very hard to accept, is that at 10 PM, when my family was sleeping, a guest mistakenly entered our room and could not open the door. It is worth mentioning that the hotel staff did not properly check the information and directly used their key card to let that guest in. This not only disrupted our sleep but also made my family feel extremely concerned about safety and privacy.

My family experienced restless days during our stay here. The failure of the staff to verify the guest's information before taking action is a serious mistake, reflecting a lack of professionalism and poor safety management processes.

reviews.title: Vrai 5* à Tuy Hoa
reviews.positives: Très bel établissement digne d'un 5*. Magnifique villa privative, site en harmonie avec le cadre tropical. Propreté irréprochable, le personnel nettoie en permanence toutes les zones du site. Villa exceptionnelle - 200 m2, 2 niveaux, piscine privée, terrasse, jardin, confort irréprochable (Wifi - literie - SDB...). Les piscines de l'établissement sont bien tenues et gigantesques. Pas de souci pour les transats - Petit déjeuner varié et très complet avec un large choix de plats asiatiques et occidentaux. Le personnel est prévenant, souriant et discret.
reviews.negatives: La plage mal exploitée : le restaurant de bord de mer paraît abandonné. La plage est magnifique mais pas aménagée. Pas de transat à disposition des clients ni de personnel pour s'occuper des clients. Pas de maître nageur (la mer est agitée). Propreté très moyenne. Cela crève le cœur de voir un si beau site délaissé, d'ailleurs, il n'y a pas un seul client qui se baigne. Cela dit, la clientèle très majoritairement locale de l'établissement ne semble pas s'y intéresser. C'est vraiment dommage...

reviews.title: Perhaps the most cost-effective resort in Tui Hoa in March 2026!
reviews.text: I stayed at this resort during my trip to Tuy Hoa. The location is great because it is right in front of the beach, making it nice for walking along the coast and enjoying the ocean view. The room was spacious and generally clean, and the swimming pool was also nice since it is close to the beach. The staff were friendly during my stay. One thing to note is that events are sometimes held inside the resort, which can cause noise. I asked the hotel in advance, but they said events can be scheduled suddenly, so it’s not always possible to confirm beforehand. Overall, it was still a comfortable beachfront place to stay in Tuy Hoa.

reviews.title: Resort Hotel
reviews.text:
It's a little out of town, so unless you have a scooter, its a bit of a walk to any eateries or cafe. 
However, they do have a restaurant/cafe as well as room service on the premises. 

If you want to mostly explore the town/area, then I would probably choose a different hotel, but great if you want to stay within the resort. 

There are a lot of families/children, so if you want to relax by the pool, would recommend getting a villa. 

Breakfast was good, had a lot of option and varied a little day by day. 

Sea can be a bit rough depending on the season, so just look into that before coming to Tuy Hoa.

reviews.title: Resort yên tĩnh thích hợp nghĩ dưỡng
reviews.text: Resort nằm ngay trung tâm, tiện đi lại và ăn uống trong TP Tuy Hòa. Phòng ốc tốt, giường to, hồ bơi ở căn villa sạch sẽ, phòng được vệ sinh cũng tốt. Bãi biển tháng 7 rất đẹp và sạch sẽ, không sóng lớn nên trẻ nhỏ tắm biển cũng an toàn. Buffet sáng thì ở tầm vừa phải, không phải xuất sắc nhưng cũng được. Có máy pha cafe ổn nè.  Một góp ý cho Resort là nhân viên ở bàn reception/check-in/out.  Các bạn nên được huấn luyện kỹ hơn trong nghành hospitality, ví dụ khi khách check-in các bạn đã quên mời khách welcome drinks, và khi check-out thì bạn nữ lại không khéo léo, khách đứng xếp hàng đợi check-out mà không tiếp trước, rồi 1 khách khác đi thẳng vào bàn check-out là bạn ấy tiếp luôn, không để ý có khách đang đứng chờ. Nếu được huấn luyện tốt thì ở resort tầm này các bạn sẽ biết giải quyết và ứng xử cho tốt rất dễ dàng, và cái cách bạn nv đáp lại khách cũng cho thấy không được ổn thỏa.  Tuy nhiên, các bạn nv khác (lái xe điện, dọn dẹp phòng) thì vui vẻ,  rất ok.

reviews.title: Villa has many shortcomings.
reviews.text:
I was assigned to a villa near the stairs and the children's play area, which is quite unfortunate because upstairs they have a clear view of my yard and pool. Across from me is the children's play area, so I cannot speak naturally as usual, and the trees around have few leaves, allowing me to see people walking by, and they can see me too. It seems that privacy is a major drawback of this location, so I use the yard and pool very little. Moreover, the disconnection between the two rooms is an issue. The design of this villa makes me feel distant from my friends; I am on the lower floor, and every time I want to go up to the kitchen/guest area upstairs, I need my friend's keycard from the upper floor. It feels quite strange, as if the lower floor is separate from the upper floor while being in the same villa. Therefore, our group A has to wait for group B to wake up before we can enter the kitchen, which is quite funny. Additionally, the villa is about 200 square meters, but the area I use is quite small, mostly just a few rooms, the bedroom, kitchen, and bathroom. I would say the aesthetics are high, but the convenience is low, in my opinion. 
Aside from those points, everything else is good. The staff is very friendly, the pool in the middle is beautiful, and there are many amenities like a bar. It's a bit disappointing that the karaoke is closed, and the beach has big waves, so swimming isn't possible.

reviews.title: a blissful stay
reviews.text:
My recent stay at Rosa Alba was a delightful experience filled with warmth and comfort. This impeccable customer service truly stood out, with the staff's attentiveness and genuine hospitality making my stay pleasant. 

The room was spacious offering ample space to relax and unwind after a long day of sightseeing. 

The breakfast buffet was very impressive with a changing menu each day, especially the chef's bar.  The salad bar was filled with fresh vegetables. 

To sum up, Rosa Alba met my expectations with its exceptional service, spacious room, and delicious breakfast offerings. It's a great choice for a getaway, especially when you travel with young children.

reviews.title: We should've had a perfect stay
reviews.text:
Rosa Alba has a great location where you can go straight to its private beach through a built-in tunnel. 

We should've had a perfect stay, except for 3 things:
1. The toilet was clogged: we had to call for the housekeeping twice during our stays, the flush didn't function well in once (no litters thrown out). They couldn't do anything with it, the final answer we got is we had to be patient and flush multiple times.
2. The built-in tunnel: it was literally in the basement with the karaoke and night club, so if you want to go the beach in the evening, it's might be a bit awkward.
3. Venue for rent: they had corp guests rented for gala dinner by the pool and did not have any announcement about it. It was SUPER ANNOYING, they were too loud and the pool was occupied the whole night.

reviews.title: Perfect location, but not suitable for elderly or disability
reviews.text:
The resort is well maintained, staffs are friendly and helpful. The restaurant is well-priced. The location couldn’t be better, just 5 mins drive to the city centre. 
However, there are a couple of improvement needed. Firstly, it is not friendly for elderly since there are so many stairs. If you need to go the beach, you’d need to go through the long, dark, and wet tunnel. At first, we didn’t think it’d be a big deal, but there are other areas with a lot stairs as well. So think about it when you make the booking, esp. if you have elderly or pram. 
Secondly, there is no sound insulation on the windows. So we can hear all the screaming at the pool even though I stayed at 5th floor!

reviews.title: Rosa Alba Resort in Tuy Hoa
reviews.text:
Rosa Alba is a very nice resort with a fresh water swimming pool and a salt water pool situated right by the beach. Unfortunately the ocean was rough and windy in January. 
The staff was very good and friendly specially Ms. Tra My, Hằng, and Dung at the breakfast area. The breakfast food was average with few choices. 
Avoid peak hours between 8-9 am due to heavy traffic and noisy kids. 
Bell boys Mr Hửơng and Đạt are super helpful. The spa massage was fairly good with 30% promo discount thanks to the good advice of Ms. Trân who took time to explain the menu and offered such good custumer service!
The hotel is a bit remote from town.  Save some taxi money by renting a motorbike from Thiên Vũ a trustful bike/auto rental company. It’s really easy to roam around Tuy Hòa ơn bike.

reviews.title: Beautiful and clean resort, but needs soundproofing
reviews.text:
We booked a two-bedroom villa for a 3-night stay.  

Pros: The resort and villa are nicely designed, aircon system is very good.  Bed room and bath room are comfortable and well-equipped.  Swimming pools are beautiful. Good food. Breakfast menus are changed everyday. Staffs are friendly.

Cons: The kitchen doesn't have necessary appliances, no curtains surrounding the living room.

We ate at the restaurant for dinner, the selection was good and prices were reasonable for a resort. The breakfast buffet was very good, with many options for both Vietnamese and western tastes.

reviews.title: Spacious rooms & great breakfast buffet
reviews.text: My second 4-night stay in Phu Yen and first time at Rosa Alba with my elderly parents & the whole extended family with kids (a villa & a suite), and it's still a great experience. Good food, clean beach, lots of sunshine, beautiful nature & sights, reasonable price and nice people. Rosa Alba's Executive Suite is a great choice for a family of 3-4 with marvelous ocean view & spacious room. The villa's bedroom is a bit smaller but has a private swimming pool and living room for around 10-people gathering. The rooms are very clean with high ceiling, we slept very well. We enjoyed the private beach with a safe guard staff until 6pm, many swimming pool options, and lots of greens. The kids loved the small playground with trampoline & foosball (soccer table). Staff are polite, hardworking, responsible, and they are pure & honest like many other Phu Yen people, not as diplomatic as other tourist cities. A big thump up for the breakfast buffet too, well-served & offering lots of local & fusion choices/tastes with good quality. The only drawback is we cannot cook in the villa, alhthough there is a kitchen, while my dad is on a special medical diet. It took a while for me to convince the staff to let me bring our tiny rice cooker to cook for him, which is very uncommon for villas where people are supposed to cook, drink & BBQ all the time. Other than that, everything else was great & we enjoyed every moment of our 5-day stay at Rosa Alba. Will be back next time!

reviews.title: A wonderful stay
reviews.text:
We stayed at the 2 bedroom villa for 3 nights and we would've loved to stay more. The rooms were great, spacious, and clean, with all the basic necessities that you would need. Our villa was right next to the pool, the restaurant and the reception, so close to everything it's perfect for our 2 young children. What I love most about the stay was the staff, very friendly, very helpful and accommodating. I also particularly enjoy the soft music played at the lawn and 'dan da' (a kind of stone instrument) performance every night. Overall, a perfect retreat for our family.

Some cons:
- The sea is across the beach, so if you were to go there, you need to pass through a tunnel, which is rather steep and long, and it can be slippery, especially for the elderly.
- It takes quite a lot of stairs to reach the reception, so once again, not very convenient for the elderly (my mom) and babies (with strollers), although you can easily call for the buggy to pick you up.

reviews.title: I am quite satisfied with the vacation here.
reviews.text:
The hotel and the grounds are very beautiful, with a large swimming pool. For me, having an underground walkway connecting to the beach and the saltwater pool is both a plus and a minus. I was quite excited the first time, but next time I really want to walk above ground to get to the beach, as it would be much more convenient, instead of going through the tunnel which is quite exhausting, especially when going with small children and elderly people. However, currently, access is only possible through the tunnel. 
Another downside was that the night I stayed was quite noisy. I learned that there was a large group of guests also staying there at that time. However, at night, when it was already 10 or 11 PM, having loud activities in shared spaces that affect others is not good. I closed all the room doors, but there was still quite a lot of noise affecting me. 
Other than that, everything was good; the staff was enthusiastic and professional. My family flew with Vietnam Airlines, and the flight reported a delay quite late, however, the hotel still helped and allowed us to stay until 2 PM instead of 12 PM at no extra charge. I am very thankful for this.

reviews.title: A wonderful place
reviews.text: This place is amazing with a design featuring multiple swimming pools. Walking through the tunnel leads to a beautiful beach, and the restaurant offers affordable prices comparable to local eateries while providing 5-star service. Therefore, it’s best to eat rice dishes or hot pot here instead of going for fresh seafood, as it is both cheap and delicious. Although the water in Tuy Hoa, Phu Yen is colder than in other regions, making beach swimming chilly, the service and facilities of the hotel are excellent for the price. I will return soon.

reviews.title: The best value accommodation for those in need of relaxation.
reviews.text:
Compared to Da Nang or Nha Trang, the prices are unreasonable, and the quiet and spacious landscaping perfectly fit the vacation destination concept. If you take a taxi into the city, you can feel the atmosphere of local life and good restaurants without it being too busy. We love swimming, so we spent the whole day playing in the water while having meals at the restaurant next to the pool. When we went out for a run at 6 AM, the well-maintained roads were lively with people moving around and locals getting ready to start their day by soaking in the cool sea water. After running and swimming in the sea, we went back to the hotel pool to play all day long; it was a true vacation for our three-day, two-night stay.  
The staff here are quite friendly, especially a young woman named HANG who works at the restaurant. When we asked for restaurant recommendations, she kindly took the time to write down a list of good places and suggested attractions in her handwriting. We are very grateful, and the service from the kind Vietnamese people made our vacation enjoyable. Please note that it might be a bit dull for those who are not into the vacation + swimming concept!

Отель вошел в наше положение (мы хотели уехать из за погоды) и вернул деньги за последние три дня пребывания.
reviews.negatives: Вид на море проходит через дорогу. В принципе все было бы хорошо если бы не погода. Был шторм и холодно.  Еще было бы не плохо посуду на кухне оставлять. Первоначально она была совсем пустая. Но мы позвонили на рецепшн и нам принесли тарелки ложки и стаканы. И не забирали потом.

reviews.title: See you again, Phu Yen.
reviews.text: I just had a wonderful experience in Phu Yen, and Rosa Alba Resort is one of them! A 5-star hotel with a spacious area, plenty of greenery, a large clean swimming pool, a tunnel leading to the beach, a beautiful clean beach, and enthusiastic and attentive hotel staff. I stayed in a basic hotel room, which was quite spacious, airy, with a sea view, and I especially want to praise the breakfast at Rosa, which had many dishes, and most of them were delicious. I hope Phu Yen in general and Rosa in particular will become even more viral in the coming time.

reviews.title: Meget bra opphold med høy grad av service fra alt personal.
reviews.positives: Oppmerksomme og vennlige var alt personal uansett hvilken setting vi traff dem i. Rent område og villaer. Flotte villaer med basseng. Rent basseng. To flotte restauranter med nydelig utsikt til basseng og fellesområde. Stort og flott basseng på fellesområdet. Store flotte fellesområder med solsenger, sittegrupper og hengekøye. God og trygg gangvei til stranda. Fin strand. God mat på frokostbuffe og lunch/middagsrestaurant. De som ønsket det fikk kniv og gaffel til måltidene. Gode puter og dyner i sengene. Stort flott bad. Romservice. Enkel og grei utsjekking av hotellet. Veldig flott transport til flyplassen. Personalet kjørte baggasjen til villaen ved ankomst og hentet den da vi skulle sjekke ut.
reviews.negatives: Litt for hard seng. Ikke opplyst om den private stranden var fri for havslanger. Ved servering av middag/lunch ble ikke maten servert samtidig til alle rundt bordet. Vi var 7 gjester. Noen var ferdig med å spise før de siste rundt bordet fikk maten sin.

reviews.title: Excellent Service
reviews.text:
My family spend 3 days 2 nights at Rosa Alba Resort during the Vietnamese National Day Holidays. We were extremely pleased with its cozy asmosphere and friendliness.  All the service Staff were helpful, respectful and friendly.  I especially appreciate the Security Guard who nicely took photos for  my family upon our check-out.
Furthermore, good location by the beach, clean swimming pools, spacious rooms with nice seaview,  delicious food, especially the restaurants and dishes were nicely decorated with Vietnamese National Day theme during the holidays, which made our stay truly memorable.

reviews.title: Excellent 👌.
reviews.positives:
A luxury resort that goes beyond the norm. Everything is top notch and I would advice everyone to give it a try. The staff is really really helpful in every way possible. The property is kept in good shape and all around the clock you see workers maintaining to keep it in excellent shape. 

If you wanna experience a luxury stay this is the place to be. But… if you wanna experience Vietnamese culture this isn’t the place to be. The only contact you’l get with Vietnamese culture is the middle class Vietnamese who are upping their game.
reviews.negatives:
Well, they’ve got these rules. You can’t take anything from outside into the property. No food deliveries or takeaways from outside because they need to make money with their own restaurant and things (restaurant is excellent by the way). I get that, but fining people for breaking the rules? Not making me happy. 

Another one is their laundry service. Steep prices.  Cleaning a single item can easily cost you 120k but outside you get 3,5kg done for 90k. Go figure. (Do wonder if they outsource to the local laundry shops 😂, good margin though.)

reviews.title: Deserving to come back!
reviews.text:
The resort features rooms with stunning ocean views, and the facilities are very new, providing a comfortable and pleasant experience throughout the stay. I was assisted with an early check-in, and I was particularly impressed with Ms. Tra Mi – a very friendly GRO who was always attentive and proactively inquired to support the customers. This left a great impression on me and is definitely a plus point to consider returning in the future.

In addition, the resort has many beautiful photo spots, most notably the super luxurious pool – which looks extremely impressive in photos. Overall, the experience here deserves a perfect score of 10/10.

reviews.text: I have visited this place a few times already. The space is clean and convenient, and every corner is perfect for taking photos. What I like the most is the way the staff, the uncles and aunts, clean the rooms or the parking area; everyone is very enthusiastic, and when they see you, they smile and bow their heads in greeting, which is very endearing. The reception staff was also very friendly; I accidentally booked a room with two single beds, and thanks to their help, I was able to switch to a double bed room. reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: We stayed here for 3 nights and truly enjoyed our time. The room was very clean, comfortable, and had a beautiful view. The staff were always supportive and friendly, making us feel welcome from the moment we arrived. Breakfast was excellent, with a wide range of local dishes as well as simple options for international guests, so there was always something for everyone. What we love most is the consistency – we’ve been coming back to this resort for the past 5 years, and the quality of service, hospitality, and overall experience has always remained excellent. Highly recommended for a relaxing and memorable stay!

reviews.title: Lovely stay in Rosa Alba Resort
reviews.text:
I hope that the Korean people who read my review will definitely visit! :)

It was such a happy and wonderful stay, so I'll organize my thoughts one by one.

Kindness: All the staff at the front desk, pool, restaurant, and concierge were very friendly. Communication in English is possible!

Room: As shown in the photos, the room was clean with a modern design and spacious size, making it very comfortable (the bathroom, room interior, and balcony were all spacious!!)
+ I arrived quite early at 12 o'clock, but they allowed an early check-in before 1 o'clock. I'm mentioning this for reference, as they do this if possible! 

Pool: The pool is divided into shallow and deep areas (maximum depth of 1.5m), making it great for visiting with kids, and it is quite large, perfect for water activities.
+ There’s an underground passage leading to the beach, so you can walk to the beach without feeling hot. Once you go out, it’s really quiet with beach beds and a private beach, which is just wonderful.

Restaurant: Since there aren't really any restaurants around, you have to eat at the hotel restaurant, but the menu is diverse, in-room dining is available, and compared to big cities, it’s quite affordable, which is a plus! The food was also decent. :)

Breakfast: It’s not large, but it has everything you need, so it’s satisfactory if you’re not looking for something extravagant. The coffee was really delicious, so I had to have two cups! 

Elevator: There are two in total, but they are small, so it can be a bit uncomfortable if there are many people, but honestly, I had no discomfort during my stay (I rode with other guests just once or twice while wandering around with my partner).

Room view: I was assigned a pool view room right in the center of the resort, and the view was beautiful from the 7th floor, which was just the right height.

Overall: My stay was filled with nothing but happy memories, with not a single drawback! It was a vacation spot with no Koreans at all, so it felt like I was truly abroad, and I almost wanted to keep it a secret! But I decided to leave a review anyway. :) + You can rent a motorcycle from the accommodation and go to a nearby square where there are mini motorcycles and mini carts with the long tires; it’s really fun, so I recommend it! (It starts in the evening!)

Oh, and I was able to propose beautifully in the room, even though I had to hold off on doing it in Korea, and I’m really grateful to the hotel staff who helped with the proposal, especially the restaurant supervisor! Thank you for consulting on the cake order and preparing everything on time! 🥰
